sutta: monks possessed of 6 qualities among is enough for self enough for others what 6 herein among is quick to grasp the subtle doctrines or dhamma number 2 he remembers those heard number 3 he reflects on the meaning of those remembered number 4 knowing both the letter and the spirit he walks in conformity with dhamma number 5 he has a pleasant voice a good enunciation is obeying in speech distinct free from hoarseness and informative number 6 he is 1 who instructs insights rouses and gladdens his companions in the holy life and then possessed of these 6 among is worth is enough for self enough for others just stop here for a while and just to recapitulate now um there are 6 qualities which makes some among which benefits among as well as benefits others number 1 he's quick to understand the dhamma number 2 he remembers the dhamma number 3 he reflects on the meaning of the dharma and number 4 he walks in conformity with dhamma number 5 he has a pleasant voice number 6 he is 1 who instructs and encourages his companions in the holy life now to continue likewise i'm possessed of 5 qualities among is enough for self and enough for others what 5 he is not very quick in grasping the subtle doctrines but he possesses the other 5 qualities that means he remembers the dhamma he reflects on the meaning of the dhamma remembered and then before he number 3 he walks in conformity with dhamma that he has a pleasant voice and he instructs and encourages his companions in the holy life possession of these 5 is enough for self enough for others possessed of 4 qualities among is enough for self but not for others what for he is quick to grasp the subtle doctrines he remembers those heard he reflects on those remembered knowing both the letter and the spirit he walks in conformity with dhamma but he does not have a pleasant voice nor does he instruct rouse and gladden his companions in the holy life with these 4 he is enough for self but not for others possessed of all qualities he is enough for others but not for self what for he is quick to grasp the subtle doctrines remembers them but not he does not reflect on them or walk in conformity with dhamma yet he has a pleasant voice and he instructs his companions in the holy life with these 4 he is enough for others but not for self possessed of 3 qualities he is enough for self but not for others what tree he is not quick to grasp the subtle doctrines but he remembers them he reflects upon them he walks in conformity with dhamma yet he has not a pleasant voice nor does he instruct his fellows in the holy life with these 3 he is enough for self but not for others possessed of 3 qualities he is enough for others but not for self what tree he is not quick to grasp the subtle doctrines but remembers them yet he does not reflect on them does not walk in conformity with dhamma but he has a pleasant voice and instructs his companions in the holy light with these 3 he is enough for others but not for self possessed of 2 qualities he is enough for self but not for others what 2 he is not quick to grasp the subtle doctrines he does not remember them but he reflects on them and walks in conformity with dhamma yet he has not a pleasant voice nor does he instruct and rouse and incite his companions in the holy life with enough for self but not for others monks possessed of 2 qualities among is enough for others but not for self what to hearing among is not very quick to grasp the subtle doctrines he does not remember those heard nor reflect on the meaning of those remembered knowing neither the letter nor the spirit he does not walk in conformity with dhamma but he has a pleasant voice a good enunciation is a pain in speech distinct free from hoarseness and informative he is 1 who instructs incites rouses and gladdens his companions in the holy life possessed of these 2 qualities among is enough for others but not for self that's the end of the sutta
commentary: i'm not going to go through those qualities again perhaps if you have the taper or if you remember then you can reflect on it and slowly you'll understand the next 2 times
